content: confidence: 0.88. 6 findings: (1) HIGH/BLOCKING: PROXY_ACCESS_KEY fallback attack surface — anon key extractable from APK, proxy key path should be removed now that JWT is live or require minimum entropy. (2) HIGH/BLOCKING: RLS policies use FOR ALL without explicit WITH CHECK — implicit USING reuse is correct but fragile; add explicit WITH CHECK clauses. (3) MEDIUM/ADVISORY: auth_screen generic Exception handler exposes raw toString() to UI — sanitize. (4) MEDIUM/ADVISORY: context object in Edge Function not validated before system prompt interpolation — prompt injection vector via time_of_day string. (5) LOW/ADVISORY: payload size check uses string length not byte length — underestimates for CJK/emoji. (6) LOW/ADVISORY: redundant apikey header doubles anon key exposure in logs. Strengths: secret management correct, RLS on all 3 tables, error responses sanitized, JWT validated server-side via getUser(), user_id from JWT not client-supplied, no debug logging of content in release.

confidence: 0.88. 9 findings: (1) HIGH: _FakeAuthenticatedSupabaseService returns null client — UPSERT path has zero test execution. (2) HIGH: partial failure scenario in syncPendingSessions untested (session-1 succeeds, session-2 throws). (3) HIGH: syncSession exception/FAILED path untested. (4) MEDIUM: non-atomic two-step UPSERT (session succeeds, messages fail) — no test. (5) MEDIUM: Sign Out button has no error handling, no test. (6) MEDIUM: _triggerSyncAfterEnd never reached in SessionNotifier tests (isAuthenticated not overridden to true). (7) MEDIUM: sync_providers_test uses Future.delayed(50ms) — flaky risk. (8) LOW: isAuthenticatedProvider reactivity (signed-out to signed-in transition) untested. (9) LOW: Sync Now button lacks loading state and partial-failure feedback. Edge cases: concurrent Sync Now taps, token expiry mid-sync, large message batch, pendingSyncCount during sign-out.

confidence: 0.91. 7 findings: (1) MEDIUM/DRIFT: SyncRepository and SessionDao use raw string literals for sync status instead of SyncStatus enum — type safety gap. (2) MEDIUM/COUPLING: auth_providers.dart imports session_providers.dart for environmentProvider — wrong direction, move to infrastructure_providers.dart. (3) MEDIUM/DRIFT: Supabase init failure silently swallowed — isConfigured returns true but _client will throw StateError. (4) LOW: _uploadSession non-atomic two-step UPSERT — correct as coded but needs clarifying comment. (5) LOW: _triggerSyncAfterEnd should use unawaited() for explicit fire-and-forget intent. (6) LOW: Edge Function dependency versions pinned but aging (std@0.168.0). (7) INFO: JSONB vs TEXT schema impedance between Supabase and drift — null vs empty array default mismatch. Strengths: optional auth genuinely optional, Riverpod dependency graph directionally sound, _resolveAuthOptions lazy token read is correct, SyncResult accumulation pattern clean, Edge Function auth model correct.

confidence: 0.88. 7 findings: (1) HIGH/SCALABILITY: RLS on journal_messages uses correlated subquery — O(sessions) per row, add user_id column for O(1). (2) HIGH/SCALABILITY: syncPendingSessions processes sessions sequentially — 14 calls for 7 offline days, use bounded concurrency (3). (3) MEDIUM: Edge Function creates new SupabaseClient per request for JWT validation — 50-150ms overhead, use local JWT verification with SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET. (4) MEDIUM: _uploadSession loads all messages into memory, no chunking for PostgREST row limit. (5) MEDIUM: N+1 pattern in sync status updates — batch successful session IDs into single UPDATE. (6) LOW: startSession() calls getAllSessionsByDate() full table scan for count+latest — add targeted getSessionStats(). (7) LOW: _triggerSyncAfterEnd should use unawaited() with catchError guard. Strengths: batch UPSERT per session not per message, partial index on sync_status, watchPendingSyncCount uses COUNT aggregate, pendingSyncCount gates on auth, conversationMessages built incrementally.

Phase 4 review: APPROVE-WITH-CHANGES (confidence 0.89). 4 specialists, 29 total findings (4 blocking, 15 advisory, 10 low/info). Blocking: (1) UPSERT path zero test execution — mock returns null client, (2) RLS policies need explicit WITH CHECK, (3) PROXY_ACCESS_KEY fallback attack surface evaluation needed, (4) correlated subquery in journal_messages RLS — add user_id column. Cross-specialist consensus: optional auth model correctly implemented, offline-first never compromised, secrets properly managed, provider graph directionally sound. Key advisory themes: test coverage gaps in authenticated sync paths, type safety (raw strings vs enum), Supabase init failure handling, Edge Function performance (50-150ms JWT validation overhead). Education gate: Tier 2, emphasize Apply+Analyze on auth flow, sync state machine, and Edge Function security.
